summary: Phase 27 third retest — BOTH CRITICAL backend fixes from iter_15 are VERIFIED. (1) GET /api/leads/{id} now includes `deal_room_enabled` and `deal_room_opened_at`. (2) Customer email-match access on the same endpoint correctly redacts deal_value (→0), description (→None), commission_breakdown/override (→None), sales_associate_*/referred_by_* (→None) and non-public comments. Admin GET still returns full data (deal_value=500000, description present). Frontend retest: Admin clicked 'Open Deal Room' button and UI flipped to live view in 200ms (well under 2s SLA) WITHOUT a page reload — disabled card disappeared, deal-room-tab + message-input + send-btn rendered. Customer logged in, reached /leads/<id> without redirect, sees redacted fields, sees DealRoomTab live, posted a public message successfully (message appeared in feed). Pytest: 24/25 PASS + 3 new focused retest tests PASS = 27 PASS, 1 skip (selling-partner assigned_partners path — unavailable via public schema). No new issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend fixes at server.py:421-461 (LeadResponse adds deal_room_enabled + deal_room_opened_at) and server.py:3100-3119 (redaction in get_lead when access is via email-match only) are clean, minimal, and correctly mirror the redaction logic in the /deal-room endpoint. Comments are filtered server-side to is_public=true only.

Frontend DealRoomTab.jsx localEnabled optimistic state (lines 36-37, 61) is a robust pattern — UI is not at the mercy of parent re-fetch latency. The combined `(localEnabled !== null ? localEnabled : !!lead?.deal_room_enabled)` gives both immediate optimistic flip AND eventual consistency once the parent re-fetch lands.

Note: customer still sees the full LeadDetail layout (AI Insights card, Lead Health, Follow-ups sidebar) — this is intentional per the iteration spec ('We are NOT building a separate customer-only layout in this iteration'). All sensitive numerics ARE redacted server-side. If a stricter customer layout is desired in a future iteration, gate sidebar cards by user.role === 'customer'.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase27_iter16_retest.py (NEW — 3 focused retest cases for the 2 critical fixes + admin-full-data regression)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — admin flip 200ms, customer redaction + Deal Room post all verified

seed_data_creation: Reused canonical seed lead 59ad1b49-0e02-4689-8cd9-27f1f951b239 (customer_email=john@testco.com). Toggled deal_room enabled state during admin retest. Customer posted a public deal-room message during retest.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 27 Deal Room is fully verified end-to-end. Canonical lead: 59ad1b49-0e02-4689-8cd9-27f1f951b239 (deal_room_enabled=true post-test, customer_email=john@testco.com). Pytest suites at /app/backend/tests/test_phase27_deal_room.py (25 tests) and /app/backend/tests/test_phase27_iter16_retest.py (3 tests). Use _BlockAll cookie policy when calling /auth/login from pytest (Cloudflare __cf_bm bleed). Admin flip latency measured at 200ms; if it regresses, check (a) localEnabled state in DealRoomTab.jsx and (b) deal_room_enabled in LeadResponse model.
rca of the issue: Iter 15 reported 2 CRITICAL: (1) LeadResponse missing deal_room_enabled, (2) customer email-match leaks internal fields. Iter 16 confirms both fixes landed: LeadResponse now declares the fields (server.py:460-461), enrich_lead populates them (server.py:2645-2646), and get_lead applies redaction post-enrich when is_email_match_only_customer=True (server.py:3104-3119). Frontend DealRoomTab.jsx adds localEnabled optimistic state (lines 36-37) so the admin UI flips without waiting for parent refetch. End-to-end measured admin flip: 200ms. End-to-end customer flow: lead access ok, redaction ok, deal room live, message post ok.
